findings.title: Circular import between CurriculumComposer and ImportWizardDialog
findings.why_it_matters: ImportWizardDialog imports COURSE_IMPORTED from ./CurriculumComposer, while CurriculumComposer imports ImportWizardDialog from ./ImportWizardDialog. This creates a circular dependency. The exported value is a top-level const string ('course-imported'), so ES module hoisting handles this correctly at runtime — there is no actual breakage. However, the dependency direction is backwards: the event dispatcher (ImportWizardDialog) should own the event name constant, or it should live in a shared constants file.
findings.suggestedFix: Move COURSE_IMPORTED constant to ImportWizardDialog (as the event dispatcher) or to a shared constants module, and have CurriculumComposer import it from there instead. This eliminates the circular dependency and follows the existing pattern where ImportWizardDialog owns IMPORT_WIZARD_SET_TARGET.

findings.title: batchAddCoursesToPath rollback test does not verify existing entries survive
findings.why_it_matters: The rollback test creates a path with 0 entries before attempting batchAdd. If rollback were buggy and clobbered pre-existing entries, this test would not catch it. A stronger test would seed the path with existing entries, then verify they remain after rollback.
findings.suggestedFix: Add pre-existing entries to the path before the rollback test, then assert they remain after the error is caught.

residualRisks: Orphaned IndexedDB entries possible if syncableWrite partially succeeds before failure (same pattern as existing store methods — not new)
testingGaps: No integration test verifying ImportWizard -> course-imported event -> CurriculumComposer selection flow end-to-end
r3FixesVerified: batchAddCoursesToPath persists optimistically-updated path (line 734: get().paths.find) — VERIFIED
batchAddCoursesToPath re-throws errors after rollback (line 747: throw error) — VERIFIED
